channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Cause of death determined for 3 young U.S. women found dead at Belize resort
item.description: A cause of death has been determined for three young women from the United States found dead in their hotel room at a resort in San Pedro, Belize, late last month, NBC News reported. The trio were discovered deceased on Feb.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KTLA 5

item.title: Judge weighs request to withhold investigation records in deaths of Gene Hackman and wife
item.description: A New Mexico court is weighing whether to block the disclosure of an array of records from an investigation into the deaths of actor Gene Hackman and wife, Betsy Arakawa.
item.dc:creator: Morgan Lee, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Illegal immigrant released by Biden admin indicted on murder, rape and other charges
item.description: An illegal immigrant from Honduras was indicted last week for allegedly killing a mother of five, and now faces additional charges to murder, including rape.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How a new Georgia bill could change the fate of domestic abuse survivors in prison
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Mary Favors is still plagued by nightmares from the days her husband beat her, choked her and verbally and sexually abused her. Now, she is in prison for killing him. Their disagreements often turned physical, and five times he was convicted of abusing her.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE KRAMON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Figure skater who lost both parents in DC plane crash brings world championships crowd to its feet
item.description: Maxim Naumov came to a stop in the middle of the ice, looked up at the sky and patted his heart. Then he mouthed a few words, in Russian, to his parents. “This is for you guys. You guys are with me. I love you both.
item.dc:creator: JIMMY GOLEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Gabbard, Ratcliffe 'lied repeatedly' about Signal chat messages, Sen. Bennet says
item.description: “I think they lied repeatedly to our committee and to the House Committee. Kristen, let me try to make this as simple as I can,” Bennet said.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota officials seek answers in case of graduate student detained by ICE
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — Officials in Minnesota are seeking answers in the case of a University of Minnesota graduate student who’s being detained by U.S. immigration authorities for unknown reasons.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Rain turns to ice, cuts power in Great Lakes region while Tennessee braces for wild weather
item.description: Thousands of people have no power in Wisconsin, Michigan and Indiana after freezing rain or severe storms brought down trees and power lines.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Planning to visit these national parks? You may need to make a reservation to get in
item.description: While hundreds of parks are free to enter, others use timed entry and other reservation methods to control crowds — but that also means you may need to secure your spot well in advance.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Delta Air Lines drops new set of pilot trading cards: How to get yours
item.description: Trading card collectors and aviation fanatics will soon have an exclusive set of cards to begin collecting on Delta Air Lines flights this year.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, Fox 5

item.title: Transgender people are about 1% of the US population. Yet they're a political lightning rod
item.description: On the campaign trail, Donald Trump used contentiousness around transgender people's access to sports and bathrooms to fire up conservative voters and sway undecideds.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL and JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Duke's great D sends Blue Devils to their 18th Final Four with an 85-65 win over Alabama
item.description: Duke freshman Cooper Flagg swooped across the paint and swatted an Alabama player’s shot completely off the court. The ball landed in the stands. Flagg and the Blue Devils -- they’re headed to the Final Four.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Walter Clayton Jr. rallies Florida into Final Four with 84-79 March Madness win over Texas Tech
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Coach Todd Golden cut the final strand of the net, waved it to the crowd and performed the famed Gator Chomp. A celebration that seemed completely improbable just a few minutes earlier was in high gear. Florida is heading back to the Final Four. Walter Clayton Jr.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Hailey Van Lith scores 26 points to lead TCU to 1st Elite Eight with 71-62 win over Notre Dame
item.description: TCU is in unchartered territory thanks to Hailey Van Lith, reaching the Elite Eight for the first time.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Betts, top overall seed UCLA look for March Madness success in Elite Eight meeting with LSU
item.description: With back-to-back games of at least 30 points and 10 or more rebounds, UCLA center Lauren Betts certainly appears unstoppable. But, LSU has thwarted the Bruins before — in the Sweet 16 of last years women’s NCAA Tournament. They'll get a rematch on Sunday, this time in the Elite Eight.
item.dc:creator: ANNE M. PETERSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The Long Island iced tea may not be from that Long Island
item.description: There's likely a very specific Long Island you think of when you hear "Long Island iced tea." You may, however, be wrong.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4

item.title: Myanmar's earthquake death toll jumps to 1,644 as more bodies are recovered from the rubble
item.description: Myanmar’s ruling military confirmed death toll from a devastating 7.7 magnitude earthquake rose to 1,644, as more bodies were pulled from the rubble of the scores of buildings that collapsed.
item.dc:creator: David Rising, KRON4

item.title: Transgender teens fight back against Trump's ban on participation in girls' sports
item.description: CONCORD, N.H. (AP) — Parker Tirrell, 16, enjoys her art classes, scrolling on TikTok and working at her new job at a pet store. But most of all, the transgender teen loves playing soccer. Until last year, that wasn’t a problem.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK and RODRIQUE NGOWI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New wave of smaller, cheaper nuclear reactors sends US states racing to attract the industry
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — With the promise of newer, cheaper nuclear power on the horizon, U.S. states are vying to position themselves to build and supply the industry's next generation as policymakers consider expanding subsidies and paving over regulatory obstacles.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Musk says xAI buys social media platform X for $45 billion
item.description: Elon Musk said on Friday his artificial intelligence firm xAI has bought his social media platform X.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, ABC 4

item.title: Wall Street tumbles on worries about slower economy, inflation
item.description: Another wipeout is swamping Wall Street Friday on worries about a potentially toxic mix of worsening inflation and a U.S. economy slowing because of households afraid to spend due to the global trade war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: A mayor's lawsuit shut down a newspaper over fake stories. Now he faces corruption allegations
item.description: EVERETT, Mass. (AP) — For years, the mayor of a Boston suburb dreaded Wednesdays. That was the day when a local weekly would publish shocking allegations that he was on the take, sexually harassing women or under investigation by the FBI.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: McConnell: US headed for ‘Russia wins, America loses’ headline on Ukraine deal
item.description: Former Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell warned Thursday that U.S. negotiators trying to end the war in Ukraine are headed for a “Russia wins, America loses” headline.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Trump holds 'productive call' with new Canadian prime minister
item.description: President Trump spoke with Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney on Friday for the first time since Carney was elected earlier in the month amid tensions between the two nations.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Trump order targets 'divisive narratives' in Smithsonian museums
item.description: President Donald Trump on Thursday signed an executive order seeking to exert more control over what is displayed at Smithsonian museums and federal sites, citing what the White House called “divisive narratives” promoted by certain museums.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Powerful earthquake rocks Thailand and Myanmar, kills more than 150
item.description: A 7.7 magnitude earthquake rocked Thailand and neighboring Myanmar midday on Friday, burying an unknown number when a high-rise building under construction collapsed.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: How this auto union leader's support for Trump's tariffs scrambled labor politics again
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump has once again scrambled the politics of organized labor and the working class with his planned tariffs on auto imports.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW and STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Battle over proxy voting for new parents intensifies in House GOP
item.description: The battle between Rep. Anna Paulina Luna (R-Fla.) and House GOP leaders over looming floor action to allow proxy voting for new parents is intensifying as both sides of the heated debate dig in their heels — with the clash potentially coming to a head as soon as next week.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Luigi Mangione may get a laptop in jail to work on his defense in CEO murder case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A judge cleared the way Thursday for Luigi Mangione to get a laptop behind bars — if his jailers agree — so he can examine documents and other material in the UnitedHealthcare CEO murder case.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Violin thief-turned-bank robber sentenced to 4 years in prison
item.description: A California man convicted of orchestrating a scheme to steal valuable violins, and then robbing a bank when he realized federal investigators were closing in on him, has been sentenced.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: Sundance Film Festival is leaving Utah for Colorado, starting in 2027
item.description: Boulder has officially been unveiled as the city to host the Sundance Film Festival starting in 2027, according to a press release from the Sundance Institute.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Kristensen, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Prosecutors in the killings of 4 Idaho students seek to use man's crime scene paper as evidence
item.description: Prosecutors in the case of a man charged in the killings of four University of Idaho students are seeking to use as evidence a college paper he wrote that shows his knowledge of crime scenes, according to a court filing this week Bryan Kohberger, 30, is accused in the stabbing deaths of Ethan Chapin
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump officials' war planning group chat breach
item.description: Washington was rocked Monday by a truly extraordinary story. The editor in chief of The Atlantic, Jeffrey Goldberg, recounted how he had — apparently inadvertently — been added to a group chat featuring some of the most senior members of the United States government.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, WGN 9
